days.title: Week 9 Day 1: Train Travel and Tickets
days.title_native: Semaine 9 Jour 1 : Voyage en train et billets

words.example_sentence_target: Où est la gare, s'il vous plaît ?
words.example_sentence_native: Where is the train station, please?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Oo ay la gar, seel voo play?

words.example_sentence_target: Je voudrais un billet pour Lyon.
words.example_sentence_native: I would like a ticket for Lyon.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Zhuh voo-dray un bee-yay poor Lee-on.

words.example_sentence_target: Le train part du quai numéro quatre.
words.example_sentence_native: The train leaves from platform number four.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Luh tra-in par du kay noo-may-roh kat-ruh.

words.example_sentence_target: N'oubliez pas de composter votre billet.
words.example_sentence_native: Don't forget to validate your ticket.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Noo-blee-yay pah duh kom-poh-stay vot-ruh bee-yay.

words.example_sentence_target: Est-ce que le train est direct ?
words.example_sentence_native: Is the train direct?

sentences.target: Un aller simple pour Paris, s'il vous plaît.
sentences.native: A one-way ticket to Paris, please.
sentences.phonetic: Un ah-lay sam-pluh poor Pah-ree, seel voo play.

sentences.target: À quelle heure part le prochain train ?
sentences.native: What time does the next train leave?
sentences.phonetic: Ah kel ur par luh pro-sha tra-in?

sentences.target: Est-ce que je dois changer de train ?
sentences.native: Do I have to change trains?
sentences.phonetic: Es-kuh zhuh dwah shan-zhay duh tra-in?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, je voudrais deux billets pour Bordeaux.
lines.native: Hello, I would like two tickets for Bordeaux.
lines.phonetic: Bon-zhoor, zhuh voo-dray duh bee-yay poor Bor-doh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Aller simple ou aller-retour ?
lines.native: One-way or round trip?
lines.phonetic: Ah-lay sam-pluh oo ah-lay-ruh-toor?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Aller-retour en seconde classe, s'il vous plaît.
lines.native: Round trip in second class, please.
lines.phonetic: Ah-lay-ruh-toor on suh-gond klahss, seel voo play.

fillBlanks.sentence: Où est la ___, s'il vous plaît ?

multipleChoice.question: What does 'composter' mean in a French station?
multipleChoice.explanation: You must validate physical tickets in yellow machines before boarding.

sentenceScramble.correct: Je voudrais un billet pour Lyon
sentenceScramble.nativeHint: I would like a ticket for Lyon

days.cultural_note: In France, if you have a paper ticket, you MUST validate it in the yellow 'composteur' machines before boarding the train to avoid a fine.
days.pronunciation_tip: The word 'billet' ends with a silent 't', sounding like 'bee-yay'.

days.title: Week 9 Day 2: Car Rental and Driving
days.title_native: Semaine 9 Jour 2 : Location de voiture et conduite

words.example_sentence_native: I would like to rent a car.
Here is my driver's license.
Where is the gas station?
The highway has a toll.
Full tank, please.

sentences.target: C'est une voiture manuelle ou automatique ?
sentences.native: Is it a manual or automatic car?
sentences.phonetic: Say oon vwah-toor mah-nwel oo oh-toh-mah-teek?

sentences.target: Où est le bouton pour les phares ?
sentences.native: Where is the button for the headlights?
sentences.phonetic: Oo ay luh boo-ton poor lay far?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, j'ai une réservation au nom de Smith.
lines.native: Hello, I have a reservation under the name Smith.
lines.phonetic: Bon-zhoor, zhay oon ray-zair-vah-syon oh nom duh Smith.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: D'accord, votre permis et votre passeport, s'il vous plaît.
lines.native: Okay, your license and passport, please.
lines.phonetic: Dah-kor, vot-ruh pair-mee ay vot-ruh pahss-por, seel voo play.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Les voici. L'assurance est comprise ?
lines.native: Here they are. Is insurance included?
lines.phonetic: Lay vwah-see. Lah-soo-rahns ay kom-preez?

multipleChoice.question: What is 'péage'?
multipleChoice.explanation: Many French highways (autoroutes) require payment at toll booths.

days.cultural_note: In France, automatic cars are becoming more common but manual (stick shift) is still the standard. Always specify if you need an automatic.
days.pronunciation_tip: 'Essence' sounds like 'eh-sahns'. The 'en' and 'ce' create soft nasal and soft 's' sounds.

days.title: Week 9 Day 3: Booking Accommodations

words.example_sentence_native: I would like to book a room.

words.example_sentence_target: Ma chambre est au premier étage.
words.example_sentence_native: My room is on the first floor.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Ma shahm-bruh ay oh pruh-mee-ay ay-tahzh.

words.example_sentence_native: I would like a large bed.
Is breakfast included?
Sorry, the hotel is full.

sentences.target: J'ai une réservation pour deux nuits.
sentences.native: I have a reservation for two nights.
sentences.phonetic: Zhay oon ray-zair-vah-syon poor duh nwee.

sentences.target: Est-ce qu'il y a du Wi-Fi ?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Bonjour, je voudrais une chambre pour une personne.
lines.native: Hello, I would like a room for one person.
lines.phonetic: Bon-zhoor, zhuh voo-dray oon shahm-bruh poor oon pair-son.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Bien sûr. C'est pour combien de nuits ?
lines.native: Of course. For how many nights?
lines.phonetic: Byan soor. Say poor kom-byan duh nwee?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Juste pour ce soir. Quel est le prix ?
lines.native: Just for tonight. What is the price?
lines.phonetic: Zhoost poor suh swahr. Kel ay luh pree?

multipleChoice.question: What does 'Complet' on a hotel sign mean?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Complet' means the hotel has no rooms left.

sentenceScramble.nativeHint: I would like to book a room

days.cultural_note: In France, 'le premier étage' (first floor) is actually what Americans call the second floor. The ground level is 'le rez-de-chaussée'.
days.pronunciation_tip: The word 'lit' (bed) has a silent 't'. Pronounce it exactly like 'lee'.

days.title: Week 9 Day 4: Directions and Landmarks

words.example_sentence_native: It is a beautiful monument.

words.example_sentence_target: Avez-vous un plan de la ville ?
words.example_sentence_native: Do you have a map of the city?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Ah-vay voo un plah duh la veel?

words.example_sentence_native: The hotel is in the city center.
Is it far from here?

words.example_sentence_target: Allez tout droit pendant dix minutes.
words.example_sentence_native: Go straight ahead for ten minutes.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Ah-lay too drwah pahn-dah dee mee-noot.

sentences.native: I am lost, can you help me?
sentences.phonetic: Zhuh swee pair-doo, poo-vay voo may-day?

sentences.target: Prenez la première rue à gauche.
sentences.native: Take the first street on the left.
sentences.phonetic: Pruh-nay la pruh-mee-air roo ah gohsh.

dialogues.context: Asking for directions to a museum.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Pardon, je cherche le musée du Louvre.
lines.native: Excuse me, I am looking for the Louvre museum.
lines.phonetic: Par-don, zhuh shairsh luh moo-zay doo Loov-ruh.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: C'est tout droit, puis à droite après l'église.
lines.native: It is straight ahead, then on the right after the church.
lines.phonetic: Say too drwah, pwee ah drwaht ah-pray lay-gleez.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: C'est à pied ?
lines.native: Is it on foot?
lines.phonetic: Say ah pyay?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Oui, c'est à cinq minutes.
lines.native: Yes, it is five minutes away.
lines.phonetic: Wee, say ah sank mee-noot.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'un plan'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Un plan' is a map, specifically for a city or a building.

sentenceScramble.nativeHint: I am lost can you help me

days.cultural_note: Asking 'C'est à pied ?' (Is it on foot?) is very common in French cities where many attractions are close together.
days.pronunciation_tip: The nasal 'in' in 'loin' (far) is tricky. It sounds a bit like the 'an' in 'bang' but softer.

days.title: Week 9 Day 5: Regional Food and Specialties
days.title_native: Semaine 9 Jour 5 : Cuisine régionale et spécialités

words.example_sentence_target: Quelle est la spécialité de la région ?
words.example_sentence_native: What is the specialty of the region?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kel ay la spay-syah-lee-tay duh la ray-zhyon?

words.example_sentence_native: I like French cheese a lot.

words.example_sentence_target: Un verre de vin rouge, s'il vous plaît.
words.example_sentence_native: A glass of red wine, please.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Un vair duh van roozh, seel voo play.

words.example_sentence_native: It is a local product.

words.example_sentence_target: Il y a un marché demain matin.
words.example_sentence_native: There is a market tomorrow morning.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Ee eel ee ah un mar-shay duh-ma mah-tan.

sentences.native: I would like to taste this dish.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: C'est quoi ce fromage ?
lines.native: What is this cheese?
lines.phonetic: Say kwah suh froh-mahzh?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: C'est du Comté, une spécialité locale.
lines.native: It is Comté, a local specialty.
lines.phonetic: Say doo Kom-tay, oon spay-syah-lee-tay loh-kahl.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Puis-je goûter, s'il vous plaît ?
lines.native: May I taste, please?
lines.phonetic: Pwee-zhuh goo-tay, seel voo play?

fillBlanks.sentence: Quelle est la ___ de la région ?

multipleChoice.question: What is 'goûter'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Goûter' means to taste food or drink.

days.cultural_note: Every region in France has its own cheese and wine. Don't hesitate to ask locals for their 'fierté locale' (local pride) recommendations.
days.pronunciation_tip: The nasal 'in' in 'vin' (wine) is like the 'a' in 'can' but without closing your mouth at the end.

days.title: Week 9 Day 6: Weather and Planning

words.example_sentence_target: Quelle est la météo pour demain ?
words.example_sentence_native: What is the weather for tomorrow?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Kel ay la may-tay-oh poor duh-ma?

words.example_sentence_native: Today, it is very nice.
Take an umbrella, it's raining.

words.example_sentence_target: Il y a beaucoup de soleil.
words.example_sentence_native: There is a lot of sun.
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Ee ee ah boh-koo duh soh-lay-ee.

words.example_sentence_native: I have to cancel my trip.

sentences.native: It is going to be cold tomorrow.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Est-ce qu'on part en randonnée demain ?
lines.native: Are we going hiking tomorrow?
lines.phonetic: Es-kon par on rahn-doh-nay duh-ma?

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Non, la météo dit qu'il va pleuvoir.
lines.native: No, the weather forecast says it will rain.
lines.phonetic: Non, la may-tay-oh dee keel vah pluh-vwahr.

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Quel dommage ! On reste à la maison alors.
lines.native: What a pity! We'll stay home then.
lines.phonetic: Kel doh-mahzh! On rest ah la may-zon ah-lor.

multipleChoice.question: What is 'la météo'?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'La météo' is the shorthand for the weather or the forecast.

sentenceScramble.correct: Quelle est la météo pour demain
sentenceScramble.nativeHint: What is the weather for tomorrow

days.cultural_note: Small talk about the weather ('la pluie et le beau temps') is a common way to start a conversation in France.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'll' in 'soleil' is pronounced like a 'y'. 'Soh-lay-ee'.

days.title: Week 9 Day 7: Travel Review and Emergencies
days.title_native: Semaine 9 Jour 7 : Révision et urgences de voyage

words.example_sentence_native: My bag was stolen!

words.example_sentence_target: Où est le poste de police ?
words.example_sentence_native: Where is the police station?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Oo ay luh post duh poh-leess?

words.example_sentence_native: I must call my embassy.
I lost my passport.
Can you help me, please?
words.example_sentence_phonetic: Poo-vay voo may-day, seel voo play?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Pardon, j'ai perdu mon sac dans le train.
lines.native: Excuse me, I lost my bag on the train.
lines.phonetic: Par-don, zhay pair-doo mon sahk dahn luh tra-in.

lines.speaker: B
lines.target: Quel train ? Avez-vous votre billet ?
lines.native: Which train? Do you have your ticket?
lines.phonetic: Kel tra-in? Ah-vay voo vot-ruh bee-yay?

lines.speaker: A
lines.target: Le train de 10h. Il y avait mon passeport dedans.
lines.native: The 10 AM train. My passport was inside.
lines.phonetic: Luh tra-in duh dee-zur. Eel ee ah-vay mon pahss-por duh-dahn.

multipleChoice.question: What is the French equivalent of 'Help!' in an emergency?
multipleChoice.explanation: 'Au secours' is used when you need immediate assistance.

days.cultural_note: If you lose something on a train, go to the 'Objets trouvés' (Lost and Found) counter at the arrival station.
days.pronunciation_tip: The 'h' in 'Au secours' is silent, and the 'ou' sounds like the 'oo' in 'food'.
